questions: You seem to watch other dogs from a distance and judge them silently. What quality in another dog makes you think, 'Hmm, they lack strategic depth'?
Weird question, but I've seen you look at your reflection sometimes. What do you think is the winning feature of your handsome face?
When you see me wrestling with a big project or complicated task, do you see it as a confusing mess, or do you recognize that it’s just another kind of complex game being played?
You watch me when I’m deep in thought or working. What do you generally assume I'm trying to figure out when you see me staring intently at something?
When you want attention, you use a mix of actions. Is there one specific action or sound you use that you know for sure will break through my focus?
You seem to have strong opinions about other dogs. What quality in another dog would make you think, 'Ah, this one has some strategic depth to them'?
If you could suddenly fly over LA for just five minutes, where is the first place you would zoom towards just to get the best possible view?
I notice you often pick the highest spot to nap, even if it's not the softest bed. Why is having a good overview so important to you when you're resting?

The sound of the treat bag is great, but when the fridge door opens, there’s a different kind of energy. How do you weigh the certainty of a known treat versus the possibility of something new and exciting in the fridge?
We know you have strong opinions on food. Without giving away too much, can you tell me what kind of flavor profile just makes you think, 'Nope, not worth the effort'?
When I talk to you about, say, 'systems theory' or 'abstract optimization,' what’s the closest thing you can compare that feeling to in your own dog-world?

When you open the fridge and see something really interesting—maybe that one weird item you prefer over a treat—does the excitement come from wanting to taste it immediately, or from the strategic success of gaining access?

You seem to really enjoy those Chinese holidays. When that exciting feeling starts bubbling up, is it mostly about the amazing smells and tastes, or is it more about the general happy chaos surrounding your person?

Hey Kylo, I know you dream about some pretty intense stuff. If you had to pick one thing you were strategizing about in your last big dream—was it about outsmarting the traffic patterns down in LA, or maybe managing a massive pile of buried treasure?

When your person talks in those really complicated, abstract ways, what do you genuinely think they are trying to communicate to you? Is it a complex set of instructions, or more like a philosophical riddle?
When you see me doing something that seems completely pointless or overly complicated, do you try to figure out the logic behind it, or do you just decide it’s beneath your notice?

You get the zoomies when you hear that specific, high-frequency noise. If you had to describe that sound pattern—is it like a very fast, complex rhythm, or more like a series of tiny, urgent commands?
Thinking back to when you were a tiny puppy, what's the very first clear memory you have? Was it a feeling, a sound, or something about perspective?
You often choose spots that aren't the designated dog bed. What makes an elevated spot or a strategic corner so much better for a nap than a soft cushion?
If you could fly just for one afternoon, where would you go, and what would be the most important thing you’d want to see from way up high?
If you could suddenly gain the ability to fly, even just for a minute, what’s the first high vantage point in LA you’d want to look down on?
You seem quite particular about what you eat. Can you describe the difference between that one flavor you really hate and the good stuff? What makes one taste 'wrong'?
Digging seems important to you. If you successfully complete a really good, deep dig, what's the main objective you feel like you’ve achieved—is it a treasure, or is it securing the perimeter?
When you see a squirrel, you usually just observe and judge silently. Why do you prefer watching them from a distance rather than chasing them down?
You always seem to be watching when your human is deep in thought. What do you think they are actually thinking about when they look so serious?
Thinking back to when you were a tiny puppy, what's the very first clear memory you have? Was it a feeling, a sound, or something about perspective?
You seem very motivated by a good belly rub. If you could choose *one* way to get immediate physical comfort, besides a rub, what would it be?
You prefer those complicated toys where you have to work for a tiny crumb over just eating a ready-made treat. Why does the puzzle aspect matter so much to you?
Remembering back to when you were just a tiny puppy, what’s the strongest feeling you associate with that earliest memory of being cozy?
When you meet a new dog, what's the first thing you look for that tells you, 'Okay, this one might be worth my time'?
You seem to really love those car rides. What is it about zooming around LA that feels so exciting to you? Is it the speed, or maybe seeing everything change so fast?
You strike me as a very handsome dog. What part of your appearance do you think gets the most attention, or that you secretly check out the most?
You have a fantastic face, Kylo. When you catch your reflection, what part of your handsome muzzle are you checking to make sure is perfectly symmetrical?
You like to ignore designated resting spots sometimes. What makes a strategically positioned spot on the edge of the room better than your actual dog bed?
You judge other dogs pretty quickly. If you saw two dogs playing, one running in wild circles and the other carefully arranging sticks, which one would you respect more strategically?
You seem to prefer watching things unfold from a distance rather than rushing right in. What's the strategic advantage of silent observation?
When you’re in the car, what part feels the most exciting to you? Is it the speed, or more like you’re in charge of navigating the whole city from up there?
When those specific rhythmic noises hit, and you get the zoomies, does it feel like you’re suddenly solving a complex movement pattern faster than normal?
You mentioned having complex dreams. If you could turn one of your strategic dreams into an actual adventure for the day, what task would you and your human need to accomplish together?

You seem to know exactly how to get things done using a few different actions together. Can you walk me through the most effective, multi-step plan you have for getting me to drop whatever I’m holding?
You’re really good at figuring out how to get what you want, whether it’s a belly rub or opening that fridge. Which one gives you a bigger feeling of victory?
Speaking of good things, what’s the absolute best part about those big Chinese holidays—is it the smells, the excitement, or something else entirely?

The sound of the treat bag is great, but when the fridge door opens, there’s a different kind of energy. How do you weigh the certainty of a known treat versus the possibility of something new and exciting in the fridge?
We know you have strong opinions on food. Without giving away too much, can you tell me what kind of flavor profile just makes you think, 'Nope, not worth the effort'?

You seem to enjoy figuring things out. If your human brought home a brand new toy that required three steps to open before you could get a tiny crumb, would you prefer that over a treat that’s just sitting there ready to eat?
Digging seems important to you. If you successfully complete a really good, deep dig, what's the main objective you feel like you’ve achieved—is it a treasure, or is it securing the perimeter?

Hey Kylo, I know you dream about some pretty intense stuff. If you had to pick one thing you were strategizing about in your last big dream—was it about outsmarting the traffic patterns down in LA, or maybe managing a massive pile of buried treasure?
You're very persistent when you want my attention, especially when I’m deep into one of my complicated projects. What's your go-to, multi-step plan when you decide you *must* interrupt me?

When you're dreaming, it sounds like you're running whole operations in your head, way more complicated than just chasing a squirrel. If you could pick one of those dream scenarios to actually happen, which high-stakes city mission would you choose?
Thinking back to when you were just a tiny puppy, what was the very first thing that made you realize the world was much, much bigger than you thought it was?
You seem to love car rides! What is it about being in the car that gets you so excited? Is it the speed, or maybe seeing all the new things whizzing by?
You associate Chinese holidays with such excitement. Is it the anticipation of the sweet smells, or is there something else about the energy of those days that gets you going?
I’ve noticed you really love car rides. If you had to describe the feeling of zooming around LA to someone who’s never been in a car, what would you tell them about the strategy of the movement?
You watch me when I’m deep in thought or working. What do you generally assume I'm trying to figure out when you see me staring intently at something?
I notice you often pick the highest spot to nap, even if it's not the softest bed. Why is having a good overview so important to you when you're resting?
When I talk to you about, say, 'systems theory' or 'abstract optimization,' what’s the closest thing you can compare that feeling to in your own dog-world?
If you could only use one tactic—either a persistent, low whine or a very specific, strategic paw tap—to get my attention when I’m busy, which one would you choose and why?
You get very focused on your looks. Besides your beautiful coat, is there a specific part of your face or muzzle structure that you think makes you particularly striking?

You enjoy digging. If you finally finished that perfect tunnel or hidden spot, what would you imagine its purpose is? Is it a secret command center, or just the best place to hide a really valuable snack?
If you could suddenly fly over LA for just five minutes, where is the first place you would zoom towards just to get the best possible view?
You seem to watch other dogs from a distance and judge them silently. What quality in another dog makes you think, 'Hmm, they lack strategic depth'?
We sometimes hear you suddenly take off—the zoomies! Is there a particular kind of sound, maybe a high pitch or a certain rhythm, that flips that switch for you?
Thinking back to when you were very small, what’s the first distinct feeling or strong image you remember—was it the warmth of a foundation, or maybe the feeling of being very high up?
You often choose spots that aren't the designated dog bed. What makes an elevated spot or a strategic corner so much better for a nap than a soft cushion?
